Generative biology · genpi.org

Your biology,
rendered as art.

Turn your DNA or biometric data into a one-of-a-kind generative fractal — computed from the numbers that make you you, entirely on your own device.

Create yours How it works
Private by design — your data never leaves your device · no uploads · no servers · no collection
Start here

Pick your data, make your piece.

Choose the kind of data you have. Your sources and packages unfold as you go.

From data to a print you can hang.

12 × 18 in · portrait · archival
mockup room — real photographed render coming
The science

What is a fractal — and how does your data become one?

A fractal is a shape that repeats its own pattern at every scale: zoom in forever and new detail keeps unfolding. The Mandelbrot set is built by running numbers through one simple equation over and over, asking whether each point stays bounded or escapes to infinity.

GenPi takes the numbers inside your data — the variants in your DNA, or the rhythms of your sleep, heart rate and activity — and maps them onto that equation's parameters: where to look, how deep to zoom, how many iterations to run, which colors to paint.

It all runs locally, in your browser. Because your data is unlike anyone else's, so is your fractal. The image beside this is generated live, right now, on your device.

How to use it

Data to wall art in four steps.

STEP 01

Export your data

Download your raw file from 23andMe, Ancestry, Apple Health, or the Oura web portal.

STEP 02

Load it locally

Select the file. It's read right in your browser — never uploaded or stored.

STEP 03

Generate

Your unique fractal renders in seconds. Adjust the palette if you like.

STEP 04

Own it

Download, order a print, or mint your NFT — or all three.

GenPi — generative art from your biology.
25% of proceeds → Foundation for Future Aesthetics · 25% → Quantum Biology DAO
